attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	/**
2	 * Tool registry — spec §16.
3	 *
4	 * Single source of truth for the §16 floating tool palette.
5	 *
6	 *   - `TOOL_REGISTRY` lists every primary drawing tool with its spec'd
7	 *     hotkey, lucide icon name (resolved via `Icons[icon]`), and per-mode
8	 *     visibility.
9	 *   - `paletteForMode(mode)` returns the ordered tool buttons for a
10	 *     given workspace mode (Plan, 3D, Plan+3D, Section, etc.).
11	 *   - `isToolDisabled(tool, document)` lets the palette dim tools that
12	 *     can't activate (e.g. Floor before any walls exist).
13	 *
14	 * Per-tool grammar specs (location-line cycle, chain mode, sketch-vs-
15	 * pick, etc.) live in `tools/<tool>.ts` and are referenced from this
16	 * registry by the `kind` discriminator.
17	 */
18	
19	import type { TFunction } from 'i18next';
20	
21	import type { IconName } from '@bim-ai/ui';
22	
23	export type ToolId =
24	  | 'select'
25	  | 'wall'
26	  | 'door'
27	  | 'window'
28	  | 'floor'
29	  | 'roof'
30	  | 'stair'
31	  | 'railing'
32	  | 'room'
33	  | 'dimension'
34	  | 'section'
35	  | 'tag'
36	  | 'align'
37	  | 'split'
38	  | 'trim'
39	  | 'wall-join';
40	
41	/** Modify-group tool IDs — used by ToolPalette to insert a separator. */
42	export const MODIFY_TOOL_IDS = new Set<ToolId>(['align', 'split', 'trim', 'wall-join']);
43	
44	export type WorkspaceMode = 'plan' | '3d' | 'plan-3d' | 'section' | 'sheet' | 'schedule' | 'agent';
45	
46	export interface ToolDefinition {
47	  id: ToolId;
48	  label: string;
49	  icon: IconName;
50	  /** Hotkey label (the actual key handler is wired by WP-UI-D03). */
51	  hotkey: string;
52	  /** Workspace modes where this tool button shows. */
53	  modes: WorkspaceMode[];
54	  /** Optional helper text shown in the tooltip. */
55	  tooltip?: string;
56	}
57	
58	export function getToolRegistry(t: TFunction): Record<ToolId, ToolDefinition> {
59	  return {
60	    select: {
61	      id: 'select',
62	      label: t('tools.select.label'),
63	      icon: 'select',
64	      hotkey: 'V',
65	      modes: ['plan', '3d', 'plan-3d', 'section', 'sheet', 'schedule', 'agent'],
66	      tooltip: t('tools.select.tooltip'),
67	    },
68	    wall: {
69	      id: 'wall',
70	      label: t('tools.wall.label'),
71	      icon: 'wall',
72	      hotkey: 'W',
73	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
74	      tooltip: t('tools.wall.tooltip'),
75	    },
76	    door: {
77	      id: 'door',
78	      label: t('tools.door.label'),
79	      icon: 'door',
80	      hotkey: 'D',
81	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
82	      tooltip: t('tools.door.tooltip'),
83	    },
84	    window: {
85	      id: 'window',
86	      label: t('tools.window.label'),
87	      icon: 'window',
88	      hotkey: 'Shift+W',
89	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
90	      tooltip: t('tools.window.tooltip'),
91	    },
92	    floor: {
93	      id: 'floor',
94	      label: t('tools.floor.label'),
95	      icon: 'floor',
96	      hotkey: 'F',
97	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
98	      tooltip: t('tools.floor.tooltip'),
99	    },
100	    roof: {
101	      id: 'roof',
102	      label: t('tools.roof.label'),
103	      icon: 'roof',
104	      hotkey: 'R',
105	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
106	      tooltip: t('tools.roof.tooltip'),
107	    },
108	    stair: {
109	      id: 'stair',
110	      label: t('tools.stair.label'),
111	      icon: 'stair',
112	      hotkey: 'S',
113	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
114	      tooltip: t('tools.stair.tooltip'),
115	    },
116	    railing: {
117	      id: 'railing',
118	      label: t('tools.railing.label'),
119	      icon: 'railing',
120	      hotkey: 'Shift+R',
121	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d', '3d'],
122	      tooltip: t('tools.railing.tooltip'),
123	    },
124	    room: {
125	      id: 'room',
126	      label: t('tools.room.label'),
127	      icon: 'room',
128	      hotkey: 'M',
129	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
130	      tooltip: t('tools.room.tooltip'),
131	    },
132	    dimension: {
133	      id: 'dimension',
134	      label: t('tools.dimension.label'),
135	      icon: 'dimension',
136	      hotkey: 'Shift+D',
137	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d', 'section'],
138	      tooltip: t('tools.dimension.tooltip'),
139	    },
140	    section: {
141	      id: 'section',
142	      label: t('tools.section.label'),
143	      icon: 'section',
144	      hotkey: 'Shift+S',
145	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d', 'section'],
146	      tooltip: t('tools.section.tooltip'),
147	    },
148	    tag: {
149	      id: 'tag',
150	      label: t('tools.tag.label'),
151	      icon: 'tag',
152	      hotkey: 'T',
153	      modes: ['plan', 'plan-3d'],
154	      tooltip: t('tools.tag.tooltip'),
155	    },
156	    align: {
157	      id: 'align',
158	      label: t('tools.align.label'),
159	      icon: 'align',
160	      hotkey: 'AL',
161	      modes: ['plan'],
162	      tooltip: t('tools.align.tooltip'),
163	    },
164	    split: {
165	      id: 'split',
166	      label: t('tools.split.label'),
167	      icon: 'split',
168	      hotkey: 'SD',
169	      modes: ['plan'],
170	      tooltip: t('tools.split.tooltip'),
171	    },
172	    trim: {
173	      id: 'trim',
174	      label: t('tools.trim.label'),
175	      icon: 'trim',
176	      hotkey: 'TR',
177	      modes: ['plan'],
178	      tooltip: t('tools.trim.tooltip'),
179	    },
180	    'wall-join': {
181	      id: 'wall-join',
182	      label: t('tools.wallJoin.label'),
183	      icon: 'wall-join',
184	      hotkey: 'WJ',
185	      modes: ['plan'],
186	      tooltip: t('tools.wallJoin.tooltip'),
187	    },
188	  };
189	}
190	
191	const PALETTE_ORDER: ToolId[] = [
192	  'select',
193	  'wall',
194	  'door',
195	  'window',
196	  'floor',
197	  'roof',
198	  'stair',
199	  'railing',
200	  'room',
201	  'dimension',
202	  'section',
203	  'tag',
204	  'align',
205	  'split',
206	  'trim',
207	  'wall-join',
208	];
209	
210	export function paletteForMode(mode: WorkspaceMode, t: TFunction): ToolDefinition[] {
211	  const registry = getToolRegistry(t);
212	  return PALETTE_ORDER.map((id) => registry[id]).filter((tool) => tool.modes.includes(mode));
213	}
214	
215	export interface ToolDisabledContext {
216	  hasAnyWall: boolean;
217	  hasAnyFloor: boolean;
218	  hasAnySelection: boolean;
219	}
220	
221	export function isToolDisabled(
222	  toolId: ToolId,
223	  ctx: ToolDisabledContext,
224	  t: TFunction,
225	): { disabled: boolean; reason?: string } {
226	  switch (toolId) {
227	    case 'floor':
228	      if (!ctx.hasAnyWall) return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.drawWallFirst') };
229	      return { disabled: false };
230	    case 'roof':
231	      if (!ctx.hasAnyWall) return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.drawWallFirst') };
232	      return { disabled: false };
233	    case 'railing':
234	      if (!ctx.hasAnyFloor && !ctx.hasAnyWall) {
235	        return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.addStairFirst') };
236	      }
237	      return { disabled: false };
238	    case 'dimension':
239	      if (!ctx.hasAnyWall)
240	        return { disabled: true, reason: t('tools.disabled.nothingToDimension') };
241	      return { disabled: false };
242	    default:
243	      return { disabled: false };
244	  }
245	}

import type { ComponentType, SVGAttributes } from 'react';
import {
  AlertTriangle,
  AlignCenterHorizontal,
  Camera,

  Command,

  X,
} from 'lucide-react';
import {
  // Phase 1 — drawing tools
  WallIcon,
  DoorIcon,
  WindowIcon,
  FloorIcon,
  RoofIcon,
  StairIcon,
  RailingIcon,
  RoomIcon,
  DimensionIcon,
  SectionIcon,
  TagIcon,
  CurtainWallIcon,
  ColumnIcon,
  BeamIcon,
  // Phase 2 — views & annotations
  PlanViewIcon,
  SectionViewIcon,
  ElevationViewIcon,
  OrbitViewIcon,
  SheetIcon,
  ScheduleViewIcon,
  CalloutIcon,
  ViewpointIcon,

  GridLineIcon,
  LevelIcon,
  DetailLineIcon,
  // Phase 3 — organization & coordination
  FamilyIcon,
  FamilyTypeIcon,
  GroupIcon,
  AssemblyIcon,
  LinkedModelIcon,
  MaterialIcon,
  WallLayerIcon,
  PhaseIcon,
  IssueIcon,
  ClashIcon,
  ValidationRuleIcon,
  DeviationIcon,
} from '@bim-ai/icons';

/**
 * BIM AI chrome icon set — the single import surface for icon usage outside
 * the canvas. BIM-specific icons come from `@bim-ai/icons`; generic chrome
 * icons (chevrons, close, search, etc.) come from `lucide-react`.
 *
 * Default visual rules (spec §10):

/**
 * BIM AI chrome icon set — the single import surface for icon usage outside
 * the canvas. BIM-specific icons come from `@bim-ai/icons`; generic chrome
 * icons (chevrons, close, search, etc.) come from `lucide-react`.
 *
 * Default visual rules (spec §10):
 *   - Default size 16 px in dense chrome; 18 px in tool palette; 20 px in
 *     TopBar. Pass `size` prop to override.
 *   - Default stroke width 1.5; selected state 2.0.
 *   - Color inherits `currentColor` so theme tokens drive it.
 *
 * Every icon-only button must pair its icon with `aria-label` (spec §22).
 */

export type LucideLikeIcon = ComponentType<
  SVGAttributes<SVGSVGElement> & {
    size?: number | string;
    strokeWidth?: number | string;
    absoluteStrokeWidth?: boolean;
  }
>;

/** @deprecated use StairIcon from @bim-ai/icons. Kept for test backwards compat. */
export { StairIcon as StairsIcon } from '@bim-ai/icons';

/** Spec §10.1 "concept → icon" registry. Single source of truth for chrome
 * icons. Tools / shell / dialogs read from here so the assignments can be
 * audited as a unit. */
export const Icons: Record<string, LucideLikeIcon> = {
  // Selection & manipulation

  // Modify tools

  split: Scissors,

  material: MaterialIcon,
